title: AI-Powered DDoS: Attack and Defense

modules.title: Module 1: How AI Is Rewriting the DDoS Playbook — Scale, Adaptation, and Automation
modules.sub: Understanding how AI transforms DDoS from blunt force to precision weapon
modules.description: Learners examine how AI capabilities have fundamentally shifted the scale, speed, and sophistication of modern DDoS campaigns. They analyze the gap between legacy threat models and AI-augmented attack realities, and assess why conventional defenses are structurally unprepared for adaptive adversaries.

modules.title: Module 2: Anatomy of an AI-Driven DDoS — Botnets, Amplification, and Adaptive Traffic Shaping
modules.sub: Dissecting the technical components that make AI-driven attacks adaptive
modules.description: Learners break down the architecture of AI-orchestrated botnets, tracing how command-and-control logic enables real-time traffic signature mutation and amplification coordination. They map each attack component to its operational role, building a technical model of how adaptive DDoS campaigns are constructed and sustained.

modules.title: Module 3: Detection Under Fire — Using ML to Identify Intelligent Attack Patterns in Real Time
modules.sub: Applying machine learning to distinguish attacks from legitimate traffic spikes
modules.description: Learners work through ML-based detection approaches designed to surface anomalous traffic patterns even as attackers actively reshape their signatures. They evaluate detection pipelines, identify common blind spots in threshold-based systems, and decide which signals warrant escalation under time-compressed conditions.

modules.title: Module 4: Defense Architectures — Rate Limiting, Traffic Scrubbing, and AI-Assisted Mitigation Tools
modules.sub: Selecting and layering mitigation controls for volumetric and application-layer attacks
modules.description: Learners compare mitigation architectures across volumetric and application-layer attack scenarios, weighing the tradeoffs of rate limiting, scrubbing centers, and AI-assisted filtering tools. They build decision criteria for selecting and combining controls based on attack type, infrastructure constraints, and acceptable collateral impact on legitimate users.

modules.title: Module 5: Case Studies — Mirai Variants, Application-Layer Attacks, and What Failed
modules.sub: Extracting operational lessons from real-world DDoS failures and near-misses
modules.description: Learners analyze documented incidents involving Mirai-derived botnets and sophisticated application-layer campaigns, identifying the specific detection and response failures that allowed attacks to persist or escalate. They extract transferable lessons and map failure modes to gaps in their own organizations' current defenses.

modules.title: Module 6: Incident Response Playbooks for AI-Augmented DDoS Events
modules.sub: Building structured response workflows that hold up under live attack pressure
modules.description: Learners construct a modular incident response playbook tailored to AI-augmented DDoS scenarios, defining decision points, escalation triggers, and role-specific actions for each attack phase. They stress-test playbook logic against adaptive attack conditions to ensure response steps remain executable when attack behavior shifts mid-incident.

modules.title: Module 7: Red Team Exercises — Simulating Adaptive DDoS and Stress-Testing Your Defenses
modules.sub: Running adversarial simulations to expose gaps before real attackers do
modules.description: Learners design and execute red team scenarios that replicate adaptive DDoS behavior, using simulation outputs to pressure-test detection pipelines, mitigation controls, and playbook execution under realistic load. They document findings as actionable remediation items, closing the loop between defensive planning and validated operational readiness.

synopsis: When an AI-orchestrated botnet can probe your defenses, adapt its traffic signature in seconds, and saturate your infrastructure before analysts can respond, traditional rate-limiting rules become dangerously inadequate. This course equips security professionals with the technical depth to detect adaptive DDoS campaigns, evaluate modern mitigation tooling, and build resilient incident response workflows before the next attack finds the gaps.
rationale: AI is enabling DDoS attacks that dynamically evade signature-based defenses at a scale and speed that renders legacy mitigation strategies obsolete, yet most training curricula still treat DDoS as a static volumetric problem.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to distinguish AI-adaptive DDoS traffic from legitimate spikes, select appropriate mitigation controls for application-layer and volumetric variants, and execute a structured incident response playbook under live attack conditions.
source_signals: Demo: Using AI/ML & Global Threat Intelligence to Stop DDoS Attacks in Real-Time
